messages.content: "1.	Create a .doc file, double click open the file, then type in something and save it" rewrite it in Professional English
Create a document in Word format, open it by double-clicking, enter the desired text, and then save the file.
“1. Create a .xlsx file, double click open the file, then type in something and save it” rewrite it in Professional English
Generate an Excel workbook with a .xlsx extension, open it by double-clicking on the file, input the necessary information, and proceed to save the document.
provide a step how to create pdf file

Creating a PDF file can be done in various ways depending on the content you're starting with. Below is a general step-by-step guide using Microsoft Word, which is one of the common methods:

1. Open Microsoft Word and create a new document.
2. Enter the text, images, and other content that you want to include in your PDF.
3. Once your document is ready, click on the 'File' menu located in the top left corner of the Word Ribbon.
4. From the dropdown menu, select 'Save As.'
5. In the 'Save As' dialog box, choose the location where you would like to save the file.
6. Enter a file name for your document in the 'File name' field.
7. Click on the 'Save as type' dropdown menu and select 'PDF (*.pdf).'
8. Before saving, you can also click on 'Options' to set additional parameters, such as optimizing the PDF for online viewing or including ISO 19005-1 compliant (PDF/A) formatting if required.
9. Once you've adjusted your settings, click 'Save' to create the PDF file.

Your document should now be saved as a PDF in the location you selected. You can open it with any PDF reader to ensure it has been converted correctly.

This is a simple method when using Microsoft Word, but remember that there are many software applications and online services available that can create PDFs from various file formats, including images, text files, and web pages.

"User login window and find out the FortiEDR icon in Taskbar corner icons or find it out in the Task Manager" rewrite in Professional English
Upon logging into Windows, locate the FortiEDR icon among the system tray's corner icons or identify it within the Task Manager.

"1.	User login to window and find out the FortiEDR icon in Taskbar corner icons
2.	Double click the FortiEDR icon
3.	You can see the FortiEDR’s windows and showed the FortiEDR protection status" rewrite in Professional English

1. After signing into Windows, locate the FortiEDR icon in the corner icons of the taskbar.
2. Double-click on the FortiEDR icon.
3. A window will appear displaying the current protection status of FortiEDR.
"Execute outlook application or other application used of your daily operations" rewrite in Professional English
Launch the Outlook application or any other application that is integral to your daily operations.
"Verify that application can be executable" rewrite in Professional English

"Verify that user can be create document file and modify it 
1.	Create a document in Word (.doc) format, open it by double-clicking, enter the desired text, and then save the file.
2.	Create a document in Excel (.xlsx) format, open it by double-clicking, enter the desired text, and then save the file.
3.	Create a document in PowerPoint (.ppt) format, open it by double-clicking, enter the desired text, and then save the file.
4.	Create a document in text file (.txt) format, open it by double-clicking, enter the desired text, and then save the file."  rewrite in Professional English

Confirm that users have the capability to create and modify document files:

1. Generate a Word document (.doc), open it with a double-click, input the required text, and save the document.
2. Produce an Excel workbook (.xlsx), open it with a double-click, add the necessary text, and save the file.
3. Create a PowerPoint presentation (.ppt), open it with a double-click, insert the required text, and save the presentation.
4. Craft a text file (.txt), open it with a double-click, enter the text, and save the file.
"Those files can be created and modified." rewrite in Professional English
The files mentioned can be generated and edited as needed.

"Health checks  
-	Browse some website
-	Execute outlook applications 
-	Monitoring resource utilization of FortiEDR Agent" rewrite in Professional English

Health Check Procedures:
- Navigate to various websites to ensure connectivity and browser functionality.
- Launch the Outlook application to verify its operational status.
- Observe the resource usage of the FortiEDR Agent to confirm it is running efficiently.
"Launch some application that is integral to your daily operations" rewrite in Professional English and using plantesting keywords
Initiate a critical application that is essential for your routine operational testing.
"Launch applications critical to your daily operations and testing the functionality"
Initiate essential applications fundamental to your daily operations and assess their functionality.
Launch applications critical to your daily operations and test is it running normally
Start the applications that are crucial for your daily tasks and verify that they are running properly.

"Because we have 4 proxy sites, they are Japan, Australia, Singapore, and United States. FortiSASE will select the closest proxy (POP) site with the least latency based on your IP location. Staffs in Hong Kong and Japan will use Japan proxy sit" rewrite in Professional English

Due to the presence of four proxy locations—Japan, Australia, Singapore, and the United States—FortiSASE is designed to select the nearest point of presence (POP) with the lowest latency relative to your IP address. Consequently, personnel located in Hong Kong and Japan will be routed through the Japanese proxy site.
"The Internet Service Provider (ISP) will be Fortinet, and the IP location is in Japan, with the assigned IP address being 66.35.31.253." rewrite in Professional English
Fortinet will serve as the Internet Service Provider (ISP), and the IP location is situated in Japan. The allocated IP address for this location is 66.35.31.253.

"Verify that you can download the document for your daily operations 
Download file in below:
1.	For test word file (.docx) you can download a form from (gov.hk) 
2.	For test Power Point file (.ppt) you can download a form from (gov.hk)
3.	For test Excel file (xlsx / .csv) you can download a report from (gov.hk)
4.	For test .PDF file you can download a form from (gov.hk)
5.	For test .txt file you can click here to open the webpage and press CTRL + S then save it.
Find out those files from your download location." rewrite in Professional English

Confirm that you are able to download documents necessary for your daily activities. The files for testing can be acquired as follows:

1. To test a Word document (.docx), download a form from the government website (gov.hk).
2. To test a PowerPoint presentation file (.ppt), download a form from the government website (gov.hk).
3. To test an Excel workbook (either .xlsx or .csv format), download a report from the government website (gov.hk).
4. To test a PDF file, download a form from the government website (gov.hk).
5. To test a text file (.txt), navigate to the provided webpage and use the shortcut CTRL + S to save the document.

After downloading, locate these files in your designated download directory.
"It Should be 5 files there "
There should be a total of five files present.

Find out those installers from your download location.
Should be 2 installers there." rewrite in Professional English

Ensure that you are capable of downloading application installers:

1. To test a .exe file, download the Zoom application installer.
2. To test a .msi file, download the Microsoft Teams installer.

Locate these installer files in your designated download directory. There should be two installer files available.

It should automatically connected to FortiSASE
The system should establish an automatic connection to FortiSASE.

"1.	Find the hostname of the device
2.	Login to FortiSASE portal with IAM credentials
(https://portal.prod.fortisase.com/login)
3.	On the Dashboard page, click 'Managed Endpoints'
4.	You can see the endpoint devices list
5.	Find the device with the hostname
6.	Select and right click the device
7.	In the “Management Connection” and click 'Disable'" rewrite in Professional English

1. Determine the hostname of the device.
2. Access the FortiSASE portal by logging in with your IAM credentials at https://portal.prod.fortisase.com/login.
3. Navigate to the 'Managed Endpoints' section from the Dashboard page.
4. Review the list of endpoint devices displayed.
5. Locate the device that matches the hostname you identified.
6. Right-click on the selected device.
7. In the context menu, choose 'Management Connection' and then select 'Disable'.

"1.	Double check the FortiSASE agent shortcut on Windows Desktop
2.	Click the “SMAL Login” to login and connection to FortiSASE Cloud 
3.	Type in customer’s / staff’s O365 account and password
4.	Open Microsoft authenticator app on customer’s / staff’s mobile phone for multi-factor authentication
5.	Waiting the connection create successful" rewrite in Professional English

1. Verify the presence of the FortiSASE agent shortcut on the Windows Desktop.
2. Initiate a connection to FortiSASE Cloud by clicking on the "SMAL Login."
3. Enter the customer's or staff's Office 365 credentials, including account and password.
4. Activate the Microsoft Authenticator app on the customer's or staff's mobile device to proceed with multi-factor authentication.
5. Await confirmation that the connection has been successfully established.
5.	Waiting for the connection successfully established
5. Wait for the connection to be successfully established.

2. To test a PowerPoint presentation file (.ppt), download an attachment from an Outlook email.

Check your default download folder to ensure that the files have been successfully downloaded.

"I have uploaded the 2ndPart_Intermediate.pem and RootCA.pem but it still not work. Also, the 1stPart_Root.pem when I upload It to the FortiSASE it will show a duplicated alert. Not sure what happen.

Furthermore, the last time (5th December) I updated the IP address only included the egress IP of FortiSASE log server. I don't think ingress ip is usable on the log forwarding. I have provided the ingress ips below. you also can add them to firewall's ip group.

Ingress IP:
96.45.38.253,
154.52.29.34,
154.52.14.111,
154.52.23.105,
154.52.4.90 " rewrite it

I have successfully uploaded the '2ndPart_Intermediate.pem' and 'RootCA.pem' files; however, the system is not functioning as expected. Additionally, when attempting to upload the '1stPart_Root.pem' file to the FortiSASE, I receive a warning indicating that the certificate is a duplicate. The exact cause of this issue is unclear.

Moreover, during the last update on the 5th of December, I revised only the egress IP addresses pertaining to the FortiSASE log server, as I do not believe the ingress IP addresses are applicable for log forwarding purposes. Nevertheless, I have listed the ingress IP addresses below for your reference. You may consider adding them to the IP group within the firewall.

List of Ingress IP Addresses:
- 96.45.38.253
- 154.52.29.34
- 154.52.14.111
- 154.52.23.105
- 154.52.4.90
the certificate is tested separately becase thry cant not duplicate in the fortisase
The certificates are tested individually because they cannot be duplicates within the FortiSASE system.
